purpose: Internal visual-character families for qiaomu-ppt style selection. These are execution axes, not normal user-facing choices.

selection_policy.internal_axis: Choose 1 primary visual_character_family and optionally 1 counterpoint family.
selection_policy.avoid_all_styles_mode: Never present every family as a menu. Recommend a small set with a best choice, an alternative, and an avoid note.

families.label: Editorial Minimal

visual_behavior.typography: Strong type hierarchy, generous line height, restrained accent.
visual_behavior.layout: Long-form rhythm, wide margins, occasional pull quotes and sidenotes.
visual_behavior.media: Use images as proof or atmosphere, not as filler.

families.risk: Can become empty if claims are weak; require concrete proof anchors.

families.label: Warm Editorial

visual_behavior.typography: Humanist sans or refined serif pairing when Chinese readability is proven.
visual_behavior.layout: Breathing room, soft tonal surfaces, one warm accent moment per viewport.
visual_behavior.media: Portraits, artifacts, source pages, and quiet generated atmosphere.
visual_behavior.chart_policy: Readable explanatory charts with narrative captions.

families.risk: Do not drift into beige monotony; add contrast through evidence, type scale, or source imagery.

families.label: Data-Dense Pro

visual_behavior.typography: Tabular numerals, compact labels, strong hierarchy between claim and evidence.
visual_behavior.layout: Dense but ordered grids, sticky evidence zones, chart-first pages.
visual_behavior.media: Charts, tables, source screenshots, and annotated documents dominate.
visual_behavior.chart_policy: No chartjunk; every chart has a visible takeaway and source record.

families.risk: Can look like software dashboards; avoid nested cards and filler metric rows.

families.label: Technical Terminal

visual_behavior.typography: Mono is used for code, IDs, commands, or numbered evidence, not everything by default.
visual_behavior.layout: Command palette, logs, architecture strips, and code/evidence splits.
visual_behavior.media: Real code, terminal output, architecture diagrams, product screenshots, production consoles, field engineering scenes, and system cutaways.
visual_behavior.chart_policy: Operational charts, traces, timelines, and dependency maps.

families.risk: Mono-everywhere can hurt Chinese readability; keep CJK body in a readable sans. For serious technical or operational decks, avoid soft/cute/decorative treatment unless it is an explicit concept choice with a clear rationale.

families.label: Cinematic Product

visual_behavior.typography: Large claim titles, controlled contrast, strong title/image clearance.
visual_behavior.layout: Full-bleed or stage-like media, sparse foreground, deliberate section turns.
visual_behavior.media: Real product/source imagery first; generated images only for atmosphere or concept.
visual_behavior.chart_policy: Charts appear as proof chapters, not decoration.

families.risk: Never let images cover titles; enforce safe areas and clearance contracts.

families.label: Playful Creative

visual_behavior.typography: Friendly but disciplined type; avoid childish novelty fonts for serious content.
visual_behavior.layout: Varied grids, stickers only when semantic, high scanability.
visual_behavior.media: Illustrations, screenshots, icons, and examples tied to the teaching path.
visual_behavior.chart_policy: Simple visual metaphors and low-friction comparisons.

families.risk: Can become decorative icon soup; every playful object needs a role.

families.label: Premium Cultural

visual_behavior.typography: Elegant Chinese-first reading hierarchy, careful punctuation and line height.
visual_behavior.layout: Artifact pages, chapter openers, quote spreads, timelines, and context maps.
visual_behavior.media: Primary source pages, artwork, portraits, places, and generated atmosphere as a support layer.
visual_behavior.chart_policy: Use timelines, maps, and relationship diagrams before business charts.

families.risk: Do not use generic antique paper or fake seals unless they serve the source.

families.label: Soft Futurism

visual_behavior.typography: Clean, rounded but not childish; high contrast for projector readability.
visual_behavior.layout: Layered depth, product-safe focus, restrained glass only when the object demands it.
visual_behavior.media: Real product or concept renders; avoid fake interfaces and fake devices.
visual_behavior.chart_policy: Use simple system diagrams and product flow maps.

families.risk: Glassmorphism is not a default; forbid frosted-card stacks without semantic purpose.

families.label: Indie Brutalist

visual_behavior.typography: Bold display choices, intentional roughness, controlled contrast.
visual_behavior.layout: Asymmetry, poster energy, irregular but readable rhythm.
visual_behavior.media: Collage and source artifacts, never random decorative noise.
visual_behavior.chart_policy: Use strong contrast annotations, not dashboards.

families.risk: Use as counterpoint only unless the user asks for a sharp public voice.
